mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when the policy rate remains elevated.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism activates when UNRATE crosses above the 4.5% threshold.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following UNRATE > 4.5% crossings are positive in more than 50% of instances, or correlation between UNRATE and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand that reduces near-term corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days. The mechanism is triggered when RSAFS shows a contraction relative to the prior month.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following RSAFS declines are positive in more than 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated signals stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and increases cost of capital, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires simultaneous weakness in supply and persistence of high prices.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns during stagflationary episodes (INDPRO declining and CPIAUCSL > 200) are positive in more than 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 1800 thousand units combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth above 135000 thousand signals robust demand-side momentum that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. Both conditions must be met simultaneously.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns when both HOUST > 1800 and PAYEMS > 135000 are negative in more than 45% of instances, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100 thousand persons per month signals weakening labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism activates when monthly payroll growth falls below the 100k threshold.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following months of PAYEMS growth < 100k are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) declining from elevated levels while federal funds rate remains above 5.0% signals a restrictive policy stance that is beginning to achieve disinflation, reducing near-term recession risk and supporting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both conditions.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns during disinflation episodes (CPIAUCSL declining, FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) are negative in more than 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at levels below 2.0% annualized signals subtrend economic expansion that constrains corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when growth remains below the 2.0% threshold.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during periods of GDPC1 growth below 2.0% are positive in more than 55% of instances, or correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).
